children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Director of the Federal Office of Rural Health Policy (established under ), shall make grants to each State Office of Rural Health for the purpose of improving health care in rural areas.section 912 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the Secretary may not make a grant under subsection (a) unless the State office of rural health involved agrees, with respect to the costs to be incurred in carrying out the purpose described in such subsection, to provide non-Federal contributions toward such costs in an amount equal to $3 for each $1 of Federal funds provided in the grant.

children.heading: Waiver or reduction
children.label: 2 Waiver or reduction
children.text: The Secretary may waive or reduce the non-Federal contribution if the Secretary determines that requiring matching funds would limit the State office of rural health’s ability to carry out the purpose described in subsection (a).

children.heading: Determination of amount of non-Federal contribution
children.label: 3 Determination of amount of non-Federal contribution
children.text: Non-Federal contributions required in paragraph (1) may be in cash or in kind, fairly evaluated, including plant, equipment, or services. Amounts provided by the Federal Government, or services assisted or subsidized to any significant extent by the Federal Government, may not be included in determining the amount of such non-Federal contributions.

children.label: A
children.text: rural health care issues;

children.label: B
children.text: research findings relating to rural health care; and

children.label: C
children.text: innovative approaches to the delivery of health care in rural areas;
coordinating the activities carried out in the State that relate to rural health care, including providing coordination for the purpose of avoiding redundancy in such activities; and
identifying Federal and State programs regarding rural health, and providing technical assistance to public and nonprofit private entities regarding participation in such programs.

children.heading: Requirement regarding annual budget for office
children.label: d Requirement regarding annual budget for office
children.text: The Secretary may not make a grant under subsection (a) unless the State involved agrees that, for any fiscal year for which the State office of rural health receives such a grant, the office operated pursuant to subsection (a) of this section will be provided with an annual budget of not less than $150,000.

children.label: A
children.text: to provide health care (including providing cash payments regarding such care);

children.label: i
children.text: within the State to provide technical and other nonfinancial assistance under ;section 254c(f) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: under a memorandum of agreement entered into with the State office of rural health under ; orsection 254c(h) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: under a grant under ;section 254q–1 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: to purchase medical equipment, to purchase ambulances, aircraft, or other vehicles, or to purchase major communications equipment;

children.label: D
children.text: to purchase or improve real property; or

children.label: E
children.text: to carry out any activity regarding a certificate of need.

children.label: A
children.text: paying the costs of maintaining an office of rural health for purposes of subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: subject to paragraph (1)(B)(iii), paying the costs of any activity carried out with respect to recruiting and retaining health professionals to serve in rural areas of the State; and

children.label: C
children.text: providing grants and contracts to public and nonprofit private entities to carry out activities authorized in this section.

children.heading: Limit on indirect costs
children.label: 3 Limit on indirect costs
children.text: The Secretary may impose a limit of no more than 15 percent on indirect costs claimed by the recipient of the grant.
to submit to the Secretary reports or performance data containing such information as the Secretary may require regarding activities carried out under this section; and
to submit such a report or performance data not later than September 30 of each fiscal year immediately following any fiscal year for which the State office of rural health has received such a grant.

children.heading: Requirement of application
children.label: g Requirement of application
children.text: The Secretary may not make a grant under subsection (a) unless an application for the grant is submitted to the Secretary and the application is in such form, is made in such manner, and contains such agreements, assurances, and information as the Secretary determines to be necessary to carry out such subsection.

children.heading: Noncompliance
children.label: h Noncompliance
children.text: The Secretary may not make payments under subsection (a) to a State office of rural health for any fiscal year subsequent to the first fiscal year of such payments unless the Secretary determines that, for the immediately preceding fiscal year, the State office of rural health has complied with each of the agreements made by the State office of rural health under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For the purpose of making grants under subsection (a), there are authorized to be appropriated $12,500,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 2 Availability
children.text: Amounts appropriated under paragraph (1) shall remain available until expended.
